name: Found-Footage Mockumentary
description: First-person handheld occult / cryptid mockumentary in the voidstomper / analog-horror lineage. POV operator stumbles on something they shouldn't have filmed — cult-circle discovery, photo-flash beat, the impossible 'they're now facing me' turn, retreat, and a final monster reveal. Pure diegetic English captions (3 max, lower-third), no VO, persistent REC dot + timecode + low-battery HUD overlay (Act 1) and a diegetic style-break to clean cinema register for the Act-2 reveal. Built around a 6-clip i2v pipeline with locked first-frame anchors and an absolute ban on cultist body-rotation animation (the horror is stillness — animate-the-turn and the spell breaks). Validated on a 40s 9:16 occult render at ~$27 of model spend.

slots.defaultExample: body-horror humanoid hybrid (splayed-ribcage torso + brassy-tentacle-ring eyeball head + cyst-cluster shoulders)
dense black pine forest at night, ritual clearing with bonfire
anonymous amateur hiker with a Canon Sure Shot point-and-shoot and a phone-torch — face never shown, only gloved hand visible at frame bottom

stackSummary.masterFramesAndFirstFrames: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — generate one master shot per repeated element (creature / cult circle / single grinning face / operator-hand-with-camera) using user-supplied creature reference PNGs as --ref. Then generate per-clip first-frame anchors using the relevant master shot(s) as --ref. ~$0.15 per image, plan on 8-12 images for a 5-6 clip cut. Locked-ref discipline is the single biggest determinant of cross-clip continuity.
stackSummary.i2vDefault: bytedance/seedance-2.0 via OpenRouter for the canonical Act-1 + reveal clips. Per cross-project memory (feedback_vg_model_picks.md) seedance-2.0 is the right pick for any non-default physics motion — POV walking, photo-flash hard-cut, slow whip-pan, jump-scare lunge, frozen-statue cultists while operator retreats. ~$0.14/s, 5s clips. Kling animates the body-rotation turn even when explicitly told not to; seedance honors the 'instant teleport-state, zero rotation' instruction. KNOWN: seedance privacy filter rejects photoreal-human anchors in some configs (see noski memory) — for this template that hasn't fired because faces are hooded or skull-mask uncanny rather than photoreal.
stackSummary.i2vFallback: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter — use for clips where the motion IS default-physics (slow forward POV creep through forest, simple handheld retreat). Kling's grain and chromatic aberration are slightly more believable for the Hi8 register out-of-the-box. Avoid for any clip whose action note contains the words 'instant', 'frozen', 'static', 'jump-scare' — kling will animate it anyway.
stackSummary.captionsAndOverlays: Remotion-side. 3 diegetic captions max in 40s (one establish, one peak, one end-card). Persistent HUD layer: 'REC ● MMM DD YYYY HH:MM:XX · BAT NN%' top-corners (animate the XX seconds per beat). Subtle scanline flicker + SVG chromatic-aberration filter at low opacity. Heavy VHS-glitch transition at the diegetic camera-drop. Endcard 'SIGNAL LOST' in degraded VHS font.
stackSummary.audio: ElevenLabs Music endpoint, used exclusively for SFX / ambient — NO music bed in the traditional sense. Generate: (1) sustained sub-bass horror ambient drone for entire runtime, (2) collective wet inhale for the turn beat, (3) wet-crunch-plus-multi-foot-step for the sound beat, (4) camera-drop thud + VHS signal-loss static for the act-break, (5) distant Latin-ish chant whisper layer for the cult-circle build. Every SFX prompt MUST include explicit 'NO music, NO melody, NO rhythm, NO percussion, NO singing'. ElevenLabs reliably returns pure SFX when banned this hard.
stackSummary.render: remotion-render. Render with loudnorm on (the SFX layering produces uneven peaks otherwise). VHS overlay shader runs Act-1 only (clips before the camera-drop); Act-2 reveal stays clean cinema register.

constants.structure: approach (0-12%) → discovery (12-30%) → photo + the turn (30-50%) → backing away (50-65%) → sound behind (65-80%) → monster reveal (80-100%)
constants.diegeticRule: every overlay justifies itself in-world — REC dot is because the operator is recording; timecode is because the camera burned it in; captions are because the platform added them; signal-lost is because the camera died. NEVER add a stylized title card or kinetic-typography slam — wrong vibe.
constants.stillnessRule: horror = stillness, NOT motion. Cultists are frozen statues at every distance. Animating the turn breaks the spell. The monster is static-then-explode, not constantly creeping.

variationAxes: subject_creature: body-horror hybrid / cryptid (skinwalker, wendigo) / extraterrestrial humanoid / occult demon / unknown-but-wrong
cult_location_descriptor: pine forest at night / abandoned suburb / desert ridge at dusk / coastal cliff fog / subterranean tunnel network
handheld_persona: hiker with point-and-shoot / urbex with phone / paranormal investigator with camcorder / lost dog-walker / drone operator (POV from grounded drone)
register break point: full Hi8 throughout (cheaper, slop-friendly) / Hi8 → cinema diegetic break at camera-drop (recommended) / cinema throughout (lose the VHS layer entirely)
monster reveal style: static-then-lunge / static-then-walk-stalking / never-fully-revealed (silhouette only — cheapest, often scariest)
endcard: SIGNAL LOST / TAPE EJECTED / NO SIGNAL / FOUND ON CARD #NN / blank black
doNotCopyLiterally: Specific creature anatomy details, the exact body-horror hybrid composition, the seven-cultist count, the Latin chant words, the Canon Sure Shot model. Vibe + beat structure + prompt scaffolding only.
referenceNotes: Reference-required gate (AGENTS.md rule #3) FIRES for the subject_creature in this template. The creature is the headline beat; generating from text alone produces an AI-slop blob. User MUST supply 2-3 reference PNGs that together describe the creature's anatomy (this template ships sample references for a body-horror hybrid — replace with your own creature refs before using). The cult / location / operator-hand can all be model-generated master shots; only the creature requires user references. See assets/monster-*.png for the reference style.

knownLimits.klingAnimatesTheTurn: kling-v3.0-pro will animate body rotation even when the prompt explicitly says 'instant teleport-state, zero rotation'. Use seedance-2.0 for the turn clip. Kling produced 'weird foot-in-camera walk' on POV approach in occult-mockumentary-001; seedance fixed it on regen.
knownLimits.audioInKlingForRussianBadIdea: Per memory feedback_kling_no_ru_audio.md, kling --audio is canonical for English VO only. This template ships no-VO by default — diegetic captions only — so the question doesn't arise. If you target a non-English audience, render captions in {{target_language}} and keep VO off entirely.
knownLimits.elevenlabsMusicVsSfx: Generating ambient drone + SFX via ElevenLabs Music with a NO-music ban returns clean sound-design tracks. Do NOT prompt for 'horror score' or 'cinematic music' — that triggers the music classifier and you get a melody you didn't want. Always include the 'NO music, NO melody, NO rhythm, NO percussion, NO singing' block.
